title: Scale CKC media albums for large libraries and pagination
operator_request: Capture the large-library risk found during MT-010: CKC albums and members must not load or collapse huge image sets in a way that breaks LoRA mining and album review.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Resume by testing large album counts and member pages. Do not accept a UI that only works for seeded one-image albums.

scope.summary: Make CKC media album listing and member paging safe for production-scale datasets used in image mining, character albums, and LoRA review.
base_scope: Add or verify backend album-list pagination for GET /atelier/characters/{character_internal_id}/media-albums, including offset/limit and next offset.
Ensure member paging uses bounded limits and does not expand every album with every member by default.
Fix frontend merge behavior so add-items and load-more responses preserve already-loaded members, avoid duplicates, and keep selected media stable.
Expose visible/Argus-readable loaded count, total count, next offset, and load-more status per album.
Add tests with multiple albums and more members than one page.
acceptance_criteria: Backend album listing is bounded and returns deterministic album/member page metadata for large libraries.
Frontend load-more appends or updates members without replacing previously loaded pages unless the response is explicitly a reset.
Duplicate media assets do not create duplicate visible rows when a page or add-items response overlaps existing rows.
Selected media remains selected after load-more/add-items if it still exists.
Argus can inspect page counts and trigger load-more without foregrounding the app.
risks_and_controls: Risk: one CKC character with thousands of album members stalls the backend or UI. Control: bounded backend queries and frontend page-size tests.
Risk: linking a new image collapses already-loaded pages. Control: merge-by-asset tests for add-items responses.
Risk: pagination order is unstable and agents miss or duplicate work. Control: deterministic sort and duplicate-prevention assertions.
verification: Manual or status-row inspection of loaded/total/next-offset values.

manual_requirement: Required if user/model workflow or endpoint/query shape changes.

handoff.note: Created from MT-010 backend/frontend review finding that album expansion and add-items replacement are unsafe for large libraries.
